extraction_strategy.step1: Identify 10 internal article links from the main content area (prioritize unique, non-redirecting pages; avoid disambiguation/file/etc.)
extraction_strategy.step2: For each link, visit the page and extract the exact page title (as link text) and a 1-2 sentence description from the lead paragraph that summarizes the article
extraction_strategy.step3: Assemble results into a list: {url, text, description}

details.citation_policy: All descriptions derived from visited pages; include a citation for each visited URL in final answer
details.notes: If a page redirects, follow to final URL; if a page is blocked, note as unavailable and still provide the URL and link text.

details.plan_summary: Ensure a robust result set by using alternative data within Wikipedia (lead/intro text, infobox short description) when needed to describe a page

execution_steps.step1: After collecting the 10 links (as in Candidate 1), attempt to fetch each page

execution_steps.step3: Record the reason for any fallback and provide the best available 1-2 sentence description
execution_steps.step4: Compile finalized list with available data and citations

details.verification: Cross-check all collected descriptions against the actual lead text where possible; preserve citations for the source URLs
